← Back to All Roadmaps
Cloud Security Engineer Roadmap v2025

CLOUD
SECURITY
ENGINEER

A battle-tested roadmap from zero to job-ready — covering AWS, Azure & GCP security. Built by a practitioner, for practitioners. No fluff, no filler.

12–18 months to job-ready
5 progressive phases
All 3 major clouds
Certs + hands-on labs
Learning Phases
PHASE 01FOUNDATIONS0 – 3 Months

Before touching cloud, you need a solid grounding in networking, OS fundamentals, and basic security concepts. This isn't glamorous — but skipping it will haunt you forever. Think of this as installing the firmware for everything that follows.

Networking Essentials
  • TCP/IP, OSI model — deeply, not surface level
  • DNS, DHCP, HTTP/S, TLS/SSL handshake
  • Subnetting, CIDR, routing & switching basics
  • Firewalls, NAT, VPNs, proxies
  • Packet capture with Wireshark
Operating Systems
  • Linux command line (bash scripting, file perms, processes)
  • Windows Server basics, AD fundamentals
  • Users, groups, sudo, SSH keys
  • Logs, cron jobs, systemd services
  • Package management (apt, yum, brew)
Security Basics
  • CIA Triad: Confidentiality, Integrity, Availability
  • Authentication vs Authorisation
  • Encryption fundamentals (symmetric, asymmetric, hashing)
  • PKI, certificates, CAs
  • OWASP Top 10 awareness
  • Vulnerability vs threat vs risk
Programming & Scripting
  • Python — loops, functions, APIs, file I/O
  • Bash scripting for automation
  • JSON & YAML fluency
  • Git basics (clone, commit, push, branches)
  • REST APIs — how to call them, read responses
CompTIA Security+CompTIA Network+Google IT Support Certificate
PHASE 02CLOUD CORE3 – 6 Months

Get your hands dirty with cloud infrastructure. Start with ONE cloud (AWS recommended — largest market share), get comfortable, then cross-train. Learn how cloud is architected: compute, storage, identity, networking. You need to understand what you're eventually going to secure.

Cloud Architecture Concepts
  • Shared responsibility model (critical — learn this cold)
  • Regions, Availability Zones, Edge Locations
  • Compute: VMs, containers, serverless functions
  • Storage: object, block, file storage differences
  • Databases: managed SQL vs NoSQL
  • Load balancers, auto-scaling, CDNs
Identity & Access Fundamentals
  • IAM: users, roles, groups, policies
  • Principle of Least Privilege (your religion now)
  • Service accounts vs human accounts
  • MFA everywhere — why and how
  • Federated identity, SSO, SAML, OIDC
Networking in Cloud
  • VPC / VNet / VPC concepts per cloud
  • Subnets: public vs private
  • Security Groups vs NACLs vs NSGs
  • VPN Gateway, ExpressRoute, Direct Connect
  • VPC peering, Transit Gateway, service endpoints
Infrastructure as Code
  • Terraform — write, plan, apply, destroy
  • CloudFormation (AWS) / ARM Templates (Azure)
  • Ansible basics for configuration
  • Version-controlled infra — why it matters for security
  • Secret management: never hardcode credentials
AWS Cloud PractitionerAWS Solutions Architect AssociateAZ-900 Azure FundamentalsGCP Associate Cloud Engineer
PHASE 03CLOUD SECURITY CORE6 – 10 Months

This is where you shift from cloud user to cloud security practitioner. You'll learn how attackers target cloud environments, how defenders detect and respond, and how to build security controls into cloud-native architectures. This phase is dense — take your time.

IAM Security Deep Dive
  • IAM policy analysis: condition keys, wildcards, SCPs
  • Privilege escalation paths in IAM
  • Cross-account roles and trust policies
  • AWS Organizations / Azure Management Groups
  • Permission boundaries and guardrails
  • Service control policies (SCPs)
Threat Detection & Monitoring
  • CloudTrail / Activity Log / Cloud Audit Logs
  • GuardDuty / Microsoft Defender / Security Command Center
  • SIEM integration: Splunk, Sentinel, Chronicle
  • Cloud-native SIEM: AWS Security Lake
  • Alert triage: true vs false positives
  • IOCs and threat intelligence feeds
Data Security
  • Encryption at rest: KMS / Key Vault / Cloud KMS
  • Encryption in transit: TLS enforcement
  • S3 / Blob / GCS bucket security
  • Data classification and tagging
  • DLP tools and policies
  • Database security: RDS, Aurora, Cosmos, BigQuery
Vulnerability & Posture Management
  • CSPM tools: Prisma Cloud, Wiz, Orca, Lacework
  • Security Hub / Azure Security Centre / SCC
  • Benchmark frameworks: CIS, NIST, ISO 27001
  • Misconfig detection and remediation
  • Inspector / Defender for Cloud vulnerability scanning
Container & Serverless Security
  • Docker security: image scanning, non-root users
  • Kubernetes: RBAC, network policies, Pod Security Standards
  • EKS / AKS / GKE hardening
  • Container image registries: ECR, ACR, Artifact Registry
  • Lambda / Functions / Cloud Functions security
  • Runtime security: Falco, Aqua, Sysdig
Network Security
  • WAF: AWS WAF, Azure Front Door WAF, Cloud Armor
  • DDoS protection: Shield Advanced, Azure DDoS
  • Network segmentation and micro-segmentation
  • Zero Trust Network Access (ZTNA) principles
  • PrivateLink / Private Endpoints
  • DNS security: Route 53 Resolver, Private DNS
AWS Security Specialty (SCS-C02)AZ-500 Azure Security EngineerGCP Professional Cloud Security EngineerCompTIA Cloud+CCSP (longer-term goal)
PHASE 04ADVANCED & SPECIALISE10 – 14 Months

Now you go deeper into the areas that differentiate a good candidate from a great one. Pick 1–2 specialisms to dive deep. Employers want T-shaped engineers: broad enough to operate across the stack, deep enough in something to be invaluable.

DevSecOps & CI/CD Security
  • Shift-left security: SAST, DAST, SCA in pipelines
  • GitHub Actions / GitLab CI security patterns
  • Secrets scanning: GitGuardian, truffleHog, git-secrets
  • SBOM generation and software supply chain
  • IaC security scanning: Checkov, tfsec, Semgrep
  • Secure container pipelines: Snyk, Trivy, Grype
Cloud Threat Modelling
  • STRIDE methodology applied to cloud architectures
  • Attack surface mapping in cloud environments
  • MITRE ATT&CK for Cloud matrix
  • Lateral movement and persistence techniques
  • Cloud-specific TTPs: metadata service abuse, SSRF
Incident Response in Cloud
  • IR playbooks for cloud: who does what, when
  • Forensics: memory, disk, log acquisition in cloud
  • Isolating compromised resources without data loss
  • Timeline reconstruction from CloudTrail/audit logs
  • Root cause analysis and post-mortems
Compliance & Governance
  • Frameworks: SOC 2, ISO 27001, NIST CSF, PCI DSS
  • GDPR / CCPA data residency requirements
  • AWS Config / Azure Policy / Org Policy Service
  • Automated compliance-as-code
  • Evidence collection for auditors
CCSP (ISC²)CEH or PNPT (offensive awareness)Terraform AssociateCKS — Certified Kubernetes SecurityCISM (management track)
PHASE 05JOB READY14 – 18 Months

Technical skills get you the interview. Portfolio and communication get you the job. In this phase, build proof-of-work artefacts, polish your GitHub, grind interview prep, and start applying. Don't wait until you feel "ready" — you apply your way into readiness.

Portfolio Projects
  • Build a cloud honeytoken/honeypot system and blog about it
  • Automated CSPM scanner using Python + cloud APIs
  • Terraform module with security guardrails baked in
  • Home lab: multi-cloud detection lab (AWS + Azure)
  • CTF write-ups: CloudGoat, flaws.cloud, flaws2.cloud
  • Open source contributions to cloud security tools
Interview Preparation
  • Scenario: "An S3 bucket was made public — what do you do?"
  • Whiteboard cloud architecture security reviews
  • STAR method for behavioural questions
  • Know your certs cold — examiners will probe depth
  • Practice explaining complex topics simply
Community & Network
  • LinkedIn — document your learning journey publicly
  • Write blog posts on Medium or personal site
  • Join Cloud Security Alliance (CSA) chapters
  • Attend BSides, DEF CON Cloud Village, re:Inforce
  • Engage in CloudSecWiki, r/netsec, CloudSec Slack
Cloud-Specific Security Services
☁
AWS
Amazon Web Services · ~31% market share
IAM + SCPs + Permission BoundariesIAM
GuardDutyDETECT
Security Hub + FindingsCORE
CloudTrail + AthenaAUDIT
KMS + Secrets ManagerCRYPTO
VPC Security Groups + NACLsNET
Inspector v2VULN
WAF + Shield AdvancedNET
Config + Conformance PacksCOMPLY
Macie (S3 data classification)DATA
flaws.cloud + CloudGoat labsLAB
⬡
AZURE
Microsoft Azure · ~24% market share
Entra ID (formerly AAD) + RBACIAM
Microsoft Defender for CloudDETECT
Microsoft Sentinel (SIEM)SIEM
Azure Monitor + Activity LogsAUDIT
Key Vault + Managed HSMCRYPTO
NSGs + Azure Firewall + DDoSNET
Azure Policy + BlueprintsCOMPLY
Privileged Identity Management (PIM)IAM
Private Endpoints + Service TagsNET
Conditional Access PoliciesIAM
SANS Azure CTF labsLAB
◈
GCP
Google Cloud Platform · ~11% market share
IAM + Workload Identity FederationIAM
Security Command CenterDETECT
Chronicle SIEMSIEM
Cloud Audit Logs + Log AnalyticsAUDIT
Cloud KMS + Secret ManagerCRYPTO
VPC Firewall + Hierarchical PoliciesNET
Org Policy ConstraintsCOMPLY
VPC Service ControlsNET
Cloud Armor (WAF + DDoS)NET
Binary Authorization (GKE)SUPPLY
GCP Goat + Thunder CTFLAB
Skills Depth by Role Type
🔐 Cloud Security Analyst
Threat Detection & SIEM90%
Alert Triage & IR85%
IAM & Posture Review70%
Scripting & Automation60%
Architecture Design40%
🛡 Cloud Security Engineer
Security Architecture90%
IAM & Policy Engineering85%
IaC & DevSecOps80%
Threat Detection70%
Scripting & Automation85%
🔴 Cloud Penetration Tester
IAM Privilege Escalation95%
Attack Tooling (Pacu, Prowler)85%
Cloud Misconfig Exploitation90%
Report Writing80%
Architecture Design50%
📋 GRC / Cloud Compliance
Compliance Frameworks95%
Risk Assessment90%
Policy Writing85%
Cloud Technical Knowledge60%
Stakeholder Communication90%
Job Readiness Checklist
01
Certifications
  • CompTIA Security+ achieved
  • AWS Solutions Architect Associate achieved
  • One cloud security specialty cert (SCS-C02, AZ-500, or GCP PSE)
  • Cert PDFs ready for upload on applications
02
Hands-On Evidence
  • GitHub with 3+ security projects (IaC, automation, lab write-ups)
  • Completed flaws.cloud and flaws2.cloud labs
  • Completed CloudGoat at least once
  • At least one CTF participation with write-up
  • Home lab with detection capabilities documented
03
Knowledge Depth
  • Can explain shared responsibility model cold
  • Can whiteboard a secure VPC architecture
  • Can describe an IAM privilege escalation scenario
  • Knows 5+ MITRE ATT&CK for Cloud techniques
  • Can articulate CIS Benchmark hardening steps
04
Soft Skills & Presence
  • LinkedIn profile optimised with security keywords
  • At least 2 technical blog posts published publicly
  • Active in at least one security community/Slack
  • Can explain technical risks to a business audience
  • Elevator pitch for 'why cloud security?' rehearsed
05
Interview Ready
  • 10 behavioural STAR stories prepared
  • Common cloud security scenarios practiced aloud
  • Researched target companies' cloud stack
  • Reverse questions prepared for interviewers
  • Salary band researched for your region/role
06
Target Roles to Apply For
  • Junior Cloud Security Engineer
  • Cloud Security Analyst (SOC with cloud focus)
  • DevSecOps Engineer
  • Security Operations (Cloud) Analyst
  • GRC Analyst (Cloud / SaaS focus)
  • Associate Cloud Security Consultant
Key Resources & Labs
🔴 Attack Labs
flaws.cloud & flaws2.cloud
Scott Piper's legendary AWS-specific CTF labs. Free. Non-negotiable. Do both.
🔴 Attack Labs
CloudGoat by Rhino Security
Deliberately vulnerable AWS environment. Scenario-based IAM and service exploitation practice.
📚 Learning Platform
A Cloud Guru / Pluralsight
Best structured cloud security courses with sandboxed lab environments included.
📚 Learning Platform
TryHackMe — Cloud Path
Beginner-friendly cloud security rooms. Good for building confidence before harder labs.
📖 Reading
Hacking the Cloud
hackingthe.cloud — free attacker-focused cloud TTPs wiki. Bookmark this permanently.
🛠 Open Source Tools
Prowler, ScoutSuite, Pacu
Run these against your own accounts. Read the source code. Understand what they check and why.
🎧 Podcast
Cloud Security Podcast by Google
Real practitioner conversations. Great for staying current on how professionals think.
🌐 Community
Cloud Security Alliance (CSA)
Free research papers, STAR registry, and local chapters. Join for free resources and networking.
📖 Reading
AWS Well-Architected Security Pillar
Read the actual AWS docs. Free, authoritative, directly maps to exam and job requirements.
🔴 Attack Labs
GCP Goat + Thunder CTF
GCP-specific vulnerable environments. Essential for multi-cloud exposure beyond AWS.
📺 YouTube
NetworkChuck, John Savill, fwd:cloudsec
Free, high-quality content for building cloud and security foundations from trusted creators.
📖 Books
Hacking Kubernetes + AWS Security Cookbook
Go deeper on container security and practical AWS security configurations used in production.
Cloud Security Roadmap · 2025 Edition// Learn → Build → Prove → Apply12–18 months · All clouds · Job ready
